method: L2 token-class adjacency. fraction-class = 'other' token whose raw contains fraction slash U+2044 or a vulgar-fraction char in ½¼¾⅓⅕⅙⅛⅔⅗ (E060 def). attachment = token CLASS immediately before a fraction token. R_num = frac of fractions preceded by 'num'; R_word = frac preceded by 'word'. NULL: within each line (segment between 'nl'), permute order of non-nl tokens preserving the line token multiset + nl structure; recompute rates; 2000 perms; perm p one-sided enrichment = frac(null>=obs) with add-1. SITE-CONTRAST: site-label permutation over pooled HT+Khania fractions, stat=|R_num(HT)-R_num(Khania)|, 5000 perms. Positive controls SYNTHETIC: (a) detect corpus where fractions always follow a numeral; (b) false-positive corpus where fractions placed uniformly at random within lines (40 draws).
result: Fractions are preceded by 'num' globally above the within-line shuffle null (R_num=0.387 vs null 0.130, perm p=0.0005) and by 'word' above null (R_word=0.477 vs null 0.354, perm p=0.0005). Both attachments are individually enriched in both sites, BUT the num->frac attachment is massively site-asymmetric: R_num(HT)=0.479 (enriched, p=0.0005) vs R_num(Khania)=0.055 (enriched, p=0.020), and the two sites differ significantly (site-label contrast perm p=0.0002, |diff|=0.425). Khania attaches fractions overwhelmingly to WORDS (R_word Khania ~0.67), HT attaches them roughly equally to words and numerals. The fractional attachment is therefore a SITE convention, not a uniform cross-site metrological num->frac template.
key_findings: num->frac IS enriched globally above the within-line shuffle null (R_num=0.387 vs null 0.130, perm p=0.0005), so there is a real num->frac attachment signal in the corpus as a whole.
word->frac is ALSO enriched globally (R_word=0.477 vs null 0.354, perm p=0.0005) and is in fact the MORE COMMON attachment overall (148 vs 120 num-preceded).

The num->frac attachment is strongly SITE-ASYMMETRIC: Haghia Triada R_num=0.479 (n=194, enriched p=0.0005) vs Khania R_num=0.055 (n=73, enriched p=0.020); the two sites differ significantly (site-label contrast perm p=0.0002, |diff|=0.425).

Khania attaches fractions overwhelmingly to WORDS (R_word~0.67), while Haghia Triada splits roughly evenly between words (0.41) and numerals (0.48). The fractional attachment is a SITE convention, not a uniform cross-site metrological template.
Positive control PASSED (synthetic): detect corpus flagged (p=0.0005), false-positive rate 0.05 (<=0.10) across 40 random draws; machinery is calibrated and informative.
successor_hypotheses: E071: Within Haghia Triada, is num->frac conditioned on the numeral VALUE class (round/decimal vs other) or on the preceding word-class (commodity type)? L2 token-class only.
E072: Is the Khania word->frac attachment driven by a specific small set of preceding word tokens (a closed class of fraction-bearing words), or distributed across many words? L2 type-token.
E073: Does the frac->nl entry-terminal rule (E060) hold equally at Khania given Khania's distinct attachment pattern, or does Khania also deviate on terminality? Cross-check E060 site-uniformity.

E074: Combine attachment (num->frac vs word->frac) with the logo->num rule (E060): at HT, is the full chain logo->num->frac->nl a coherent metrological template, and does Khania instead show logo->word->frac->nl?
E075: Test whether the site difference correlates with support type (tablet vs other) or context (LMIB sub-phase) rather than pure site identity — a confound check on the site-local verdict.
E076: At Khania, do the word-preceded fractions cluster on specific commodity lines (e.g., a particular product), suggesting a domain-specific (not site-wide) convention?
non_circular: fraction-class defined purely structurally (t=='other' AND raw contains U+2044 or a vulgar-fraction char) per E060, with NO fraction value, NO sign value, NO reading, NO metrological arithmetic used. attachment = the token CLASS (t field) immediately before the fraction token. The null is a within-line shuffle that preserves each line's exact token multiset and nl structure, so any enrichment is beyond chance token ordering given the line contents. Site contrast uses site-label permutation over pooled fractions, making no distributional assumptions. Positive controls are SYNTHETIC and stated as such. No adjudication by the operator; verdict is mechanical from the frozen rule.
deviations: Self-check corpus enlarged from 2 fractions to 10 (one machinery-validation detail) because with only 2 fractions the within-line null can hit the observed R_num=1.0 ~10.5% of the time by chance, making the enrichment assertion borderline; the machinery logic itself is unchanged. This is a self-check robustness fix, not an analysis change.

False-positive control run with 40 draws (instead of the minimum 20) for calibration stability; the 20-draw run gave FPR=0.10 exactly at the threshold, the 40-draw run stabilizes at 0.05 (nominal alpha). Conclusion (calibrated) is unchanged.
n_perm=2000 for global/per-site nulls and 5000 for the site contrast (above the >=1000 minimum) for p-value stability; all reported p-values are at or near the add-1 floor.
